titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John for the Byzantine Text Project
GA 2
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: Roderic L. Mullen
and members of the International Greek New Testament Project

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by Roderic Mullen and colleagues on the International Greek New Testament Project working towards an Electronic Edition of the Gospel According to John in the Byzantine Tradition, first published in 2007.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Hugh Houghton and Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

The original plain text header reads as follows: Status:G-A 2 (Basel AN IV.1) John begins Folio 194vn.b. outer margin has been trimmed since Erasmus annotated the ms.Transcribed by: RLMTranscription begun: 10.12.02Transcription finished: 14.12.02Transference to Collate by: RLMTransference to Collate begun: 10.12.02Transference to Collate finished: 14.12.02File date and time:Placed in Level1:Level1 file date and time: 14.12.02Placed in Level2: compared with paper collation and differences resolvedPlaced in Level2 by: RLMLevel2 file date and time: 11.04.03

revisionDesc.change: Converted from plain text by Hugh Houghton using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

ab.note: Erasmus did not see that he only needed to omit the lect app. egerqeis ek nekrwn and correct Thberiados, so he rewrote the whole second half of this verse
